subject: Mozi Minute: 80% Gross Margin Rule of Thumb
body:
Words I like: The best way to hit next year's goals is to not wait until next year to work on them.

Mozi Money Minute: 80% Gross Margins on Services

Too many businesses charge too little for their services. They're surprised when at the end of the month they have nothing left. And this shouldn't be a surprise. They typically look around at what everyone else charges, and offer a little more, for a little less. This is silly. 1) Because you should charge off value, not your competitors. 2) Most of your competition is broke. So don't copy them.

My Rule of Thumb: I want gross margins to be >80% (*exception for companies that sell recurring labor by the hour - think security guard services - different monster). This means if I charge $1000 per month, I want my all in cost of delivery to be LESS THAN $200. This starts me at 80% margins for my business, then I have to pay for everything else to eek out my profit. Now, if I start there, then I keep attempting to over deliver, and expand my margin as we get better. This is how you print money. It's how we've kept over 50%+ net margins in every cash flow business I've founded since the turn of the decade.

Instead of immediately thinking "this wont work for my industry" ask yourself "how could i make this work for my industry?"

In my next email - I'm going to follow up on another gross margin misconception you won't want to miss. It's especially relevant to those of you who want to scale to big #s.

As promised, money in under a minute.

Margins matter,

Alex

Managing partner Acquisition.com
